data.title: Investor Kevin O'Leary warns about Ethereum's scalability issues as transaction fees spike to $1,000, highlighting network congestion.
data.description: Prominent investor Kevin O'Leary, known for his role on "Shark Tank," expressed concerns about Ethereum's network congestion and high fees. O'Leary highlighted that Ethereum faced severe congestion, leading to transaction costs of over $1,000 for even small transactions. He compared this situation to paying a steep toll for using a congested highway. O'Leary emphasized the importance of building infrastructure capable of handling high volumes, stating that real-world adoption revealed deficiencies in Ethereum's scalability. This incident serves as a warning for all crypto investors to monitor closely as Ethereum, the largest blockchain, experiences challenges with its network performance.
EU OFFERS TO BUY US WEAPONS FOR UKRAINE USING RUSSIA’S FROZEN ASSETS The EU has offered to use part of a proposed €140bn loan backed by Russia’s frozen assets to buy US weapons for Ukraine

data.title: TAICEND in Taiwan enhances wound healing science, reducing recovery time by over 50%. 5 products showcased at 2025 MEDICA Fair in Germany.
data.description: TAICEND, a company based in Kaohsiung City, Taiwan, is making significant advancements in the field of wound healing, aiming to reduce recovery time by more than 50%. They are set to showcase their five main product series at the upcoming 2025 MEDICA Fair in Germany. Among these, the Postoperative Care Series stands out as a key offering. This development highlights TAICEND's commitment to innovation and improving healthcare outcomes on a global scale. The event in Germany will provide a platform for medical professionals and industry experts to explore these cutting-edge solutions.

data.title: Dow Jones dropped on bank fears, Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk tumbled on Trump comments. Live coverage on Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones index faced a decline due to concerns surrounding the banking sector. This drop was particularly evident in pharmaceutical companies Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk, which saw a significant decrease following comments made by President Trump. The live coverage of this event is available on Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Dow Jones futures recover with banks bouncing, but Oracle and Interactive Brokers slide; significant market movement.
data.description: Futures of the Dow Jones reversed earlier losses with regional banks showing signs of recovery, while Interactive Brokers and Oracle experienced declines due to recent updates. The shift led to Dow Jones Futures erasing losses amidst a rebound in banks, although Oracle and Interactive Brokers saw a decline in their respective positions.

data.title: EPWK introduces "Curated Goods" with 'Three Goods' standard: Good-looking, Good-to-use, and Good-surprise. Aims to break monotony.
data.description: In Xiamen, China on October 17, 2025, a group set out on a quest to discover 'EPWK Curated Goods' in search of a break from the routine of life. Their criteria for selection include passing the strict 'Three Goods' test: items must be Good-looking, Good-to-use, and deliver Good-surprises. They have a firm belief that products meeting these standards offer truly exceptional quality.

The Russian Ministry of Defense: Russian forces launched an Iskander missile strike on a Ukrainian drone launch site near Kharkiv. The strike destroyed 65 Ukrainian long-range drones, five drone launchers, and 30 militants.

data.title: NUU reveals B40 5G phone with Dual AMOLED Interface for enhanced user experience. New technology launch on Oct. 17, 2025.
data.description: Dallas-based company NUU revealed the release of the NUU B40 5G on October 17, 2025. This mobile device showcases the brand's debut Dual AMOLED Interface and Vista Display, aiming to enhance convenience, creativity, and self-expression.

data.title: Autoliv Inc. (NYSE: ALV) beats Q3 estimates with adjusted earnings of $2.32 per share, up 26% from last year. Quarterly net sales reach $2.71 billion, surpassing forecast of $2.68 billion. U.S. tariffs reduce operating margin by 20 basis points.
data.description: Autoliv Inc. (NYSE: ALV) exceeded expectations in the third quarter with a report of adjusted earnings of $2.32 per share, a 26% increase from $1.84 a year ago, surpassing analysts' estimates of $2.04. The company saw a 5.9% increase in quarterly net sales to $2.71 billion, beating the consensus of $2.68 billion. Autoliv credited this success to higher organic sales and cost efficiencies. The company reaffirmed its full-year outlook. Profitability also saw an improvement driven by organic sales growth, cost reduction initiatives, and favorable supplier settlements. Despite a hit from U.S. tariffs affecting the operating margin by roughly 20 basis points, Autoliv successfully managed this impact. Additionally, a new joint venture in China aims to innovate automotive safety electronics. Read the full story for more details.

data.title: Scotiabank anticipates CAD $74 million net income from KeyCorp in Q4 2025, with projections to rise to approximately CAD $82 million when adjusted. Conference call on December 2 for Q4 financial results will be held. The bank has assets worth around $1.4 trillion as of July 31, 2025.
data.description: Scotiabank has announced that its net income contribution from its ownership interest in KeyCorp is expected to be approximately CAD $74 million in Q4 2025. This amount represents the Bank's share of KeyCorp's Q3 2025 net income and includes acquisition-related and accounting impacts. Adjusting for the amortization of acquired intangible assets of CAD $8 million, Scotiabank's adjusted net income contribution from KeyCorp will be around CAD $82 million. The bank will release its Q4 financial results on December 2, 2025. Scotiabank, with assets of approximately $1.4 trillion as of July 31, 2025, is one of the largest banks in North America and trades on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX: BNS) and New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: BNS). For more information, visit www.scotiabank.com. Adjusted results represent a non-GAAP measure that removes specified items to better assess ongoing business performance. Forward-Looking Statements are included in the communication.

data.title: Tequity's client, Northbound, acquired by Kicksaw for contract automation. 29th Salesforce transaction marks Tequity.
data.description: In a recent strategic move, Northbound has successfully completed its 29th transaction on the Salesforce platform. The acquisition of Marks has bolstered Kicksaw's capabilities in Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) and has fast-tracked the process of quote-to-cash automation for clients utilizing Salesforce. This significant development took place in Toronto on October 17, 2025. Tequity, a prominent M&A advisory firm in North America, played a key role in facilitating this transaction.

data.title: Sen-Jam Pharm progress in addiction therapy with promising preclinical results, leading to patent filing in the US.
data.description: In Huntington, New York on October 17, 2025, Sen-Jam Pharmaceutical revealed progress in their efforts to tackle addiction by submitting a new U.S. provisional patent. The company, focused on developing cost-effective treatments for inflammation-related illnesses, took a significant stride forward in this initiative.

data.title: Top Japanese banks collaborate to issue yen-pegged stablecoin via MUFG's Progmat for faster payments and settlements.
data.description: The leading banks in Japan are collaborating to introduce a new stablecoin denominated in yen. This initiative aims to enhance the efficiency of payments and corporate settlements in the country. The stablecoin will be developed utilizing MUFG's Progmat platform, known for its advanced technological capabilities. This strategic move is part of a broader effort to modernize financial transactions in Japan while tapping into the growing popularity of stablecoins.

data.title: St. Thomas University & Archdiocese of Miami to host panel celebrating 60th anniversary of Nostra Aetate.
data.description: In Miami Gardens, Florida on October 17, 2025, St. Thomas University, in partnership with the Archdiocese of Miami and the American Jewish Committee, will hold an event titled Nostra Aetate at 60: Celebrating the Legacy of Catholic-Jewish Relations in America. The interfaith panel aims to commemorate the milestone of 60 years since the initiation of this significant event.

data.title: Jim Cramer predicts Fed will cut rates as bad bank loans spike, signaling a slowing economy. Important numbers: Zions Bancorporation NA reported bad loans incurring a sizable charge, and Western Alliance Bancorp alleged one borrower committed fraud.
data.description: Financial commentator Jim Cramer from CNBC believes that the recent increase in bad bank loans should prompt the Federal Reserve to start reducing interest rates. He stated that these credit problems are a clear indication of a weakening economy, pushing the Fed to take quick action. Cramer highlighted examples like Zions Bancorporation NA (NASDAQ: ZION) reporting significant losses from bad loans, and Western Alliance Bancorp (NYSE: WAL) uncovering borrower fraud. He stressed that credit losses typically accelerate the Fed's decision-making process due to their strong signal of economic decline. Cramer's insights are detailed in a full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Top Japanese banks collaborating to issue yen-pegged stablecoin via MUFG’s Progmat platform to modernize payments & settlements.
data.description: Major banks in Japan are collaborating to introduce a new stablecoin denominated in yen. The stablecoin will be built on MUFG’s Progmat platform and aims to enhance efficiency in payment processes and corporate settlements. This initiative reflects the growing trend of financial institutions embracing digital currencies to streamline operations.

data.title: Marcus & Millichap, Inc. (NYSE: MMI) to announce Q3 2025 financial results on Nov 7, 2025, before market opens, with a conference call at 10:30 am Eastern Time.
data.description: In Calabasas, California, Marcus & Millichap, Inc. (NYSE: MMI), a prominent brokerage firm for commercial real estate transactions, revealed plans to disclose its financial performance for the third quarter ending September 30, 2025, on November 7, 2025, before the market opens. They are scheduled to hold a webcast and conference call on the same day at 10:30 a.m. Eastern Time to analyze the results. This move aims to provide investors and stakeholders with insights into the company's financial status.
